headlines.title: What to Expect From Applied Materials' Next Quarterly Earnings Report
headlines.summary: Applied Materials is set to report its third-quarter results in August, and analysts expect a double-digit earnings increase.

headlines.title: ASML Holdings' EUV Leadership Strengthens Long-Term Growth
headlines.summary: ASML's EUV dominance and next-gen High-NA systems position it to benefit as AI demand drives advanced chip production.

headlines.title: Applied Materials (AMAT) Jumps On TSMC Expansion As Fair Value Debate Stays Open
headlines.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) moved into focus after its stock jumped 7.7% following Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing's planned chip price increases and a US$100b Arizona expansion, which underscored long-term equipment demand linked to AI infrastructure. See our latest analysis for Applied Materials. That jump sits against a strong run for Applied Materials, with the share price up 37.14% over the past 90 days and 106.

headlines.title: Texas Instruments Q2 Earnings Beat Estimates, Revenues Rise Y/Y
headlines.summary: TXN beats Q2 estimates as earnings jump 52% and revenues rise 23%, fueled by industrial, data center and automotive demand.

headlines.title: NVTS Set to Report Q2 Earnings: Buy, Sell or Hold the Stock?
headlines.summary: Navitas Semiconductor heads into second-quarter earnings on strong AI data center momentum, amid near-term revenue transition risks and a rich valuation.

description: Applied Materials, Inc. provides materials engineering solutions, equipment, services, and software to the semiconductor and related industries in the United States, China, Korea, Taiwan, Japan, Southeast Asia, Europe, and internationally. The company operates through Semiconductor Systems and Applied Global Services (AGS) segments. The Semiconductor Systems segment includes semiconductor capital equipment to enable materials engineering steps, including etch, rapid thermal processing, deposition, chemical mechanical planarization, metrology and inspection, wafer packaging, and ion implantation. The AGS segment offers integrated solutions to optimize equipment and fab performance and productivity comprising spares, upgrades, services, and 200 millimeter and other equipment and factory automation software for semiconductor and other products. It serves manufacturers of semiconductor wafers and chips, and other electronic devices. Applied Materials, Inc. was incorporated in 1967 and is headquartered in Santa Clara, California.
